name: Saint John's Prep
about: Saint John's Preparatory School is a Catholic, Benedictine, coeducational day and boarding college-preparatory school in Collegeville, Minnesota, founded in 1857 by the monks of Saint John's Abbey. It shares a campus with Saint John's University and offers both Advanced Placement courses and the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme. About a quarter of its roughly 280 students are international, coming from more than a dozen countries.
college_matriculation: Students may take college courses at affiliated Saint John's University and College of Saint Benedict; graduates attend a range of U.S. and international universities.
accreditation: ISACS (Independent Schools Association of the Central States)
notable_alumni: Eugene McCarthy, David Durenberger, George Sinner
financial_aid_notes: Need- and merit-based aid; over $1M awarded each year; merit and English-proficiency awards up to ~$15,000 for international students.

name: Shattuck-Saint Mary's
about: Shattuck-St. Mary's is an Episcopal coeducational boarding and day school in Faribault, Minnesota, founded in 1858 and serving grades 6-12 plus a post-graduate year. Its students come from more than 22 countries and 39 states, and roughly three out of four students board. The school is known for its signature programs, especially elite ice hockey and figure skating, alongside soccer, bioscience, engineering, and pre-conservatory tracks.
notable_alumni: Sidney Crosby, Nathan MacKinnon, Amanda Kessel, Fred Upton
financial_aid_notes: Nearly $7M awarded in 2023-2024; about 50% of families receive some financial aid.

name: French Camp Academy
about: French Camp Academy is an interdenominational Christian boarding school in French Camp, in rural central Mississippi, founded in 1885. Serving grades 7-12 within a Christ-centered residential environment, it places special emphasis on supporting students who lack access to resources in their home environments. Tuition is set on an income-based sliding scale, and many students pay little or nothing.
financial_aid_notes: Income-based sliding scale; nearly one-third of students pay nothing toward tuition and about two-thirds pay something but often not full tuition.

name: Mississippi School for Mathematics and Science
about: The Mississippi School for Mathematics and Science (MSMS) is the state's only public residential high school for academically gifted students, founded in 1987 and located on the campus of Mississippi University for Women in Columbus. It serves grades 11-12, with students selected statewide through a competitive application process. The curriculum emphasizes mathematics and science while also stressing the humanities, and tuition is free to admitted Mississippi students.
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free public residential school for admitted Mississippi students.

name: Mississippi School of the Arts
about: The Mississippi School of the Arts (MSA) is a two-year public residential high school in Brookhaven, Mississippi, founded in 2003 and operated by the Mississippi State Board of Education. It serves artistically gifted 11th and 12th graders selected by audition, offering advanced study in visual arts, vocal music, theatre, dance, media arts, and literary arts. The school occupies the historic Whitworth College campus, and academic coursework is taught in partnership with Brookhaven High School.
financial_aid_notes: Tuition-free; room and board fee of $500 per semester ($1,000/year) including meals.

name: Piney Woods Country Life School
about: The Piney Woods School (Piney Woods Country Life School) is a coeducational independent boarding school for grades 9-12 in rural Rankin County, Mississippi, about 21 miles south of Jackson. Founded in 1909 by Dr. Lawrence C. Jones, it is the largest of the four remaining historically African-American boarding schools in the United States. Its motto is 'Head, Heart, and Hands,' and over 98% of its graduates attend college.
notable_alumni: Helen Jones Woods, Grace Morris Allen Jones
financial_aid_notes: Tuition is income-based; institutional need-based aid covers tuition, room and board. International student tuition $7,500-$15,000.

name: Saint Stanislaus College
affiliation: Roman Catholic (Brothers of the Sacred Heart)
about: Saint Stanislaus is a Catholic college-preparatory boarding and day school for boys in grades 7-12, located on the beach in Bay St. Louis, Mississippi, about an hour east of New Orleans. Founded in 1854, it has been owned and operated by the Brothers of the Sacred Heart since its founding and sits on a 42-acre beachfront campus. The school offers 13 interscholastic sports and a student-teacher ratio of about 9:1.
religious_affiliation: Roman Catholic (Brothers of the Sacred Heart)
college_matriculation: Recent graduates compete athletically at NCAA, NAIA, and NJCAA levels; class of 2024 included a National Merit Finalist.

name: Chaminade College Preparatory School
about: Chaminade College Preparatory School is an independent Catholic college-preparatory day and resident school for boys in grades 6-12, administered by the Marianist Order in suburban Creve Coeur (St. Louis), Missouri. Founded in 1910, it opened as a boarding school that year and continues to offer 7-day, 5-day, and temporary boarding to students from across the U.S. and abroad. With roughly 870 students, it fields over 60 teams across 18 sports.
accreditation: Central States Independent Schools Association; North Central Association of Colleges and Schools
notable_alumni: Jayson Tatum, Bradley Beal, Matthew Tkachuk, Paul Stastny, Mike Kehoe
financial_aid_notes: 62% of students receive some support; over $4.9M awarded; grants $1,000-$14,000, averaging ~$9,000.
